_description: Known pitfalls and traps for year extraction. Read before touching any year data.

pitfalls.title: Land-Based Cabinet Dates
pitfalls.description: SlotCatalog and sometimes other sources return the date a game was first released as a physical slot machine cabinet, not when it went online. This is the #1 source of wrong year data.
pitfalls.example: Buffalo (Aristocrat): cabinet ~2008, online NJ 2023. SlotCatalog shows 2008.
pitfalls.mitigation: Always check provider type. For land-based-origin providers, cross-reference with SlotsLaunch which tracks online dates specifically.

pitfalls.title: January 1, 1970 (Unix Epoch Zero)
pitfalls.description: SlotsLaunch returns epoch dates for some games. Found 26 instances, mostly Wazdan and Gaming Realms.
pitfalls.mitigation: Filter out any year <= 1970 or year === 1969.

pitfalls.title: NJ Year != Global Year
pitfalls.description: NJ launch is typically 1-3 years after global online launch for European/international providers. The release_year in master is the NJ date, NOT the global date.
pitfalls.example: A game launches in Malta/UK in 2019, reaches NJ in 2021. NJ year = 2021, global year = 2019.
pitfalls.mitigation: Never use NJ year as global year for non-online-first providers.

pitfalls.title: NJ Year = Global Year (Online-First Providers)
pitfalls.description: For providers that only exist online and focus on the US market, NJ IS their primary/simultaneous market.
pitfalls.mitigation: For these providers, NJ year is a safe proxy (confidence 4).

pitfalls.title: Year-Boundary Edge Cases
pitfalls.description: A game released Dec 17, 2020 might show up as Jan 14, 2021 on SlotsLaunch. This 28-day offset crosses a year boundary.
pitfalls.example: Red Silk: GT=2020-12-17, SL=January 14, 2021. Off by 28 days but wrong year.
pitfalls.mitigation: Accept +/-1 year tolerance when SL date is in Jan/Feb and GT/NJ suggests prior December.

pitfalls.title: Game Variants Have Separate Launch Dates
pitfalls.description: Variants like 'Megajackpots Cash Eruption' have separate NJ launch dates from 'Cash Eruption'. External sources may not distinguish them.
pitfalls.mitigation: Match variants carefully. The original_release_year should reflect the base game's online launch.

pitfalls.title: Provider/Game Slug Mismatches
pitfalls.description: URL-based scraping of SlotsLaunch fails ~60% of the time because their slugs don't follow predictable patterns from game names.
pitfalls.mitigation: Use the SlotsLaunch API instead of constructing URLs. The API has proper search/filter by name.

pitfalls.title: Global Year AFTER NJ Year Is Invalid
pitfalls.description: If a source gives a global online year AFTER the NJ launch year, it's wrong. NJ IS online, so global online can't be after NJ.
pitfalls.mitigation: Filter out any result where source_year > nj_year + 1. The +1 tolerance covers timing edge cases.

pitfalls.title: SlotCatalog Is Only 70% Accurate
pitfalls.description: SlotCatalog was calibrated at 70% on AGS GT. It is the source for 1,327 existing years in master. Many of these may be wrong.
pitfalls.mitigation: Cross-reference SlotCatalog years with SlotsLaunch or slot.report. Do not trust SC alone for land-based-origin providers.
